profile-base-software-engineer.name: Software Engineer
profile-base-software-engineer.summary: Backend-oriented software engineer with 9 years of experience building APIs, application services, and internal platform tools in AWS-based environments. Rowan brings a practical mix of product delivery, automation, and operational awareness, with a track record of improving maintainability, supporting reliable releases, and partnering effectively with both product and infrastructure stakeholders. Particularly strong in TypeScript, Node.js, Python, and service integration work, Rowan is most effective on teams that value thoughtful engineering, clear ownership, and steady improvement over unnecessary complexity.
profile-base-software-engineer.coverLetter:
I am excited to apply for the {{JOB.TITLE}} role at {{JOB.COMPANY}}. My background centers on backend services, API integrations, and the delivery practices that keep software reliable in production, and I am especially interested in roles where strong engineering fundamentals matter just as much as shipping features.

Across healthcare, commerce, and managed services environments, I have built Node.js and Python services, supported data flows between internal and external systems, and improved developer workflows that help teams deliver changes with greater confidence. That experience has made me comfortable working across the full lifecycle of backend systems, from implementation and testing through deployment, observability, and production support.

What stands out to me about {{JOB.COMPANY}} is the opportunity to contribute as an engineer who can balance product needs with long-term maintainability. I enjoy working on systems that need to be understandable, dependable, and easy for other engineers to extend over time.

I would welcome the opportunity to bring that hands-on engineering approach to {{JOB.COMPANY}} and to contribute to a team building software with a strong sense of quality and ownership.

profile-base-devops-engineer.name: DevOps Engineer
profile-base-devops-engineer.summary: DevOps-focused engineer with 9 years of experience improving delivery systems, repeatable infrastructure, service reliability, and developer workflows. Rowan combines software engineering fundamentals with strong hands-on ownership of CI/CD pipelines, Terraform-based infrastructure, Kubernetes deployments, and observability tooling, and is at their best in environments where platform work directly improves the day-to-day effectiveness of engineering teams. The strongest contributions tend to be in release automation, environment consistency, incident readiness, and the practical systems thinking needed to help teams ship with more confidence.
profile-base-devops-engineer.coverLetter:
I am excited to apply for the {{JOB.TITLE}} role at {{JOB.COMPANY}}. My recent work has focused on reducing friction between engineering teams and production operations through better automation, infrastructure patterns, and release processes, and I am especially drawn to roles where DevOps is treated as an engineering discipline rather than a collection of manual handoffs.

I have built CI/CD pipelines, reusable Terraform modules, observability improvements, and deployment workflows that help teams ship faster with more confidence. In practice, that has meant creating repeatable environments, improving deployment safety, expanding monitoring and alerting, and making it easier for engineers to understand how their services behave once they reach production.

I am most interested in teams that care about reliability, clear ownership, and platform investments that genuinely improve developer experience. {{JOB.COMPANY}} appears to offer the kind of environment where those strengths would be useful.

I would welcome the opportunity to bring that DevOps and platform mindset to {{JOB.COMPANY}} and to help strengthen the systems that support both delivery speed and operational stability.

profile-base-cloud-engineer.name: Cloud Engineer
profile-base-cloud-engineer.summary: Cloud-focused engineer with 9 years of experience designing and improving AWS environments that are reliable, observable, cost-conscious, and secure. Rowan brings a practical mix of software delivery, infrastructure as code, and operational ownership to cloud platform work, with particular strength in translating broad architecture goals into workable, maintainable systems that teams can actually operate. The combination of application experience and infrastructure depth makes Rowan especially effective in cloud roles that require sound AWS judgment, thoughtful automation, and a strong eye for resiliency and usability.
profile-base-cloud-engineer.coverLetter:
I am excited to apply for the {{JOB.TITLE}} role at {{JOB.COMPANY}}. My background spans software engineering, platform automation, and AWS infrastructure design, which helps me approach cloud engineering problems from both application and operations perspectives. I am particularly interested in roles where cloud engineering means building platforms that are not only scalable and secure, but also practical for teams to understand and use well.

In recent roles, I have supported shared platforms, improved Terraform-based infrastructure patterns, strengthened observability, and partnered with teams on resilient cloud architectures that are easier to operate over time. That work has included helping define better infrastructure standards, improving visibility into production systems, and making cloud environments more consistent and supportable across teams.

What appeals to me about {{JOB.COMPANY}} is the opportunity to contribute to cloud systems that require both technical depth and sound engineering judgment. I value environments where reliability, security, and maintainability are treated as part of the design process rather than afterthoughts.

I would welcome the opportunity to contribute that cloud engineering experience at {{JOB.COMPANY}} and to help build AWS infrastructure that supports teams effectively as systems and expectations grow.

achievement-se-1.name: API Reliability Initiative
achievement-se-1.description: Improved backend service resilience by tightening error handling, expanding automated coverage, and standardizing observability for high-traffic integrations.

achievement-se-2.name: Shared Audit Platform Delivery
achievement-se-2.description: Delivered a cross-team internal service workflow that simplified audit-related engineering work and reduced duplication across product teams.

achievement-se-3.name: Testing and Release Confidence
achievement-se-3.description: Raised release confidence for backend services by improving test depth and deployment checks in ways that made production changes easier to reason about.

achievement-devops-1.name: Deployment Workflow Standardization
achievement-devops-1.description: Standardized CI/CD patterns across services so teams could ship through more consistent pipelines with fewer manual release steps.

achievement-devops-2.name: Platform Self-Service Improvement
achievement-devops-2.description: Expanded reusable automation and infrastructure patterns that reduced operational friction for engineers working across shared environments.

achievement-devops-3.name: Operational Visibility Expansion
achievement-devops-3.description: Improved alerting and dashboard coverage so rollout issues and service regressions could be detected and diagnosed faster.

achievement-cloud-1.name: AWS Environment Hardening
achievement-cloud-1.description: Strengthened shared AWS environment design through clearer access boundaries, better account structure, and more supportable operational patterns.

achievement-cloud-2.name: Cloud Resiliency Improvement
achievement-cloud-2.description: Improved reliability and recovery readiness for shared platform services by refining observability, alert tuning, and failure-response workflows.

achievement-cloud-3.name: Infrastructure Modernization Support
achievement-cloud-3.description: Helped modernize AWS-based environments with stronger Terraform patterns, clearer architecture expectations, and better day-to-day operability for engineering teams.

bullet-se-northstar-1.content: Built and maintained internal Node.js and TypeScript services that supported environment provisioning, audit workflows, and shared engineering tooling across product teams.

bullet-se-northstar-2.content: Reduced incident volume by hardening service integrations, improving test coverage, and introducing clearer operational runbooks for backend systems.

bullet-se-northstar-3.content: Led backend improvements for shared audit and compliance workflows, helping product teams rely on a smaller set of better-defined service boundaries.

bullet-se-northstar-4.content: Partnered with platform and product engineers to improve deployment safety, service observability, and day-to-day maintainability for core internal tooling.

bullet-se-northstar-5.content: Contributed to operational readiness by improving alert coverage, documenting support expectations, and tightening failure-handling behavior in production services.

bullet-se-blueridge-1.content: Delivered backend features and partner integrations for a multi-tenant commerce platform using Node.js, PostgreSQL, and event-driven workflows.

bullet-se-blueridge-2.content: Improved release confidence by expanding automated test coverage and simplifying deployment checks in GitHub Actions.

bullet-se-blueridge-3.content: Worked closely with product and support partners to stabilize partner-facing integrations and reduce recurring production issues tied to order-processing flows.

bullet-se-blueridge-4.content: Helped modernize older application components by clarifying ownership boundaries, improving SQL performance, and simplifying service-level debugging.

bullet-se-harbor-1.content: Automated recurring support and reporting tasks with Python and Bash, reducing manual operational work for the infrastructure team.

bullet-se-harbor-2.content: Assisted with Linux server maintenance, cloud account administration, and early AWS migration tasks while building a foundation in systems reliability.

bullet-se-harbor-3.content: Supported troubleshooting for customer-facing infrastructure issues and documented repeatable procedures that made operational handoffs easier for the team.

bullet-devops-northstar-1.content: Standardized CI/CD workflows and reusable deployment pipelines that shortened lead time for services moving from development to production.

bullet-devops-northstar-2.content: Built Terraform modules and platform automation that gave application teams repeatable environments, better visibility, and fewer manual release steps.

bullet-devops-northstar-3.content: Improved deployment consistency across services by defining shared release patterns, environment validation steps, and clearer rollback expectations.

bullet-devops-northstar-4.content: Expanded observability and operational dashboards so teams could diagnose rollout issues faster and better understand production behavior after releases.

bullet-devops-northstar-5.content: Worked with engineers across multiple teams to reduce manual platform work through scripting, standard templates, and improved deployment self-service.

bullet-devops-blueridge-1.content: Introduced deployment guardrails, environment validation, and rollback improvements that made releases more predictable for the engineering team.

bullet-devops-blueridge-2.content: Expanded monitoring and alerting coverage with Datadog and CloudWatch, helping engineers detect and resolve issues earlier.

bullet-devops-blueridge-3.content: Helped formalize release checklists and CI workflows so changes moved through shared environments with fewer surprises and less manual coordination.

bullet-devops-blueridge-4.content: Supported application teams by improving build feedback, deployment documentation, and the day-to-day usability of automation tooling.

bullet-devops-harbor-1.content: Automated server provisioning, patching, and routine operations tasks with shell scripting and configuration tooling.

bullet-devops-harbor-2.content: Supported on-call operations, incident triage, and infrastructure monitoring in a customer environment with strict uptime expectations.

bullet-devops-harbor-3.content: Maintained internal documentation and runbooks for recurring operational tasks, helping the team respond more consistently during support and maintenance windows.

bullet-cloud-northstar-1.content: Designed and improved AWS environments supporting shared platform services, with emphasis on reliability, least-privilege access, and operational visibility.

bullet-cloud-northstar-2.content: Partnered on Terraform-based infrastructure patterns for networking, compute, and observability used across multiple teams.

bullet-cloud-northstar-3.content: Improved AWS account structure and access patterns to make shared environments easier to operate while maintaining security and compliance expectations.

bullet-cloud-northstar-4.content: Worked on monitoring and resiliency improvements for platform services, including clearer dashboards, alert tuning, and recovery-focused operational practices.

bullet-cloud-northstar-5.content: Collaborated with application teams on practical cloud design choices around networking, identity boundaries, and service reliability tradeoffs.

bullet-cloud-blueridge-1.content: Helped modernize cloud-hosted application environments by improving deployment consistency, resource configuration, and service monitoring.

bullet-cloud-blueridge-2.content: Worked with engineers to tune database and application infrastructure for better performance, resiliency, and cost awareness.

bullet-cloud-blueridge-3.content: Supported improvements to environment consistency and release readiness as the team expanded its use of AWS-based services and shared tooling.

bullet-cloud-blueridge-4.content: Helped document infrastructure expectations and recurring operational issues so engineers could troubleshoot cloud-hosted services more effectively.

bullet-cloud-harbor-1.content: Supported foundational AWS operations including IAM administration, environment hygiene, and basic networking changes under senior guidance.

bullet-cloud-harbor-2.content: Documented operational procedures and helped execute incremental cloud migration tasks for legacy workloads.

bullet-cloud-harbor-3.content: Assisted senior engineers with account maintenance, environment reviews, and small infrastructure changes that built a strong foundation in cloud operations.

education-bullet-se-bs-1.content: Completed upper-level coursework in software engineering, data structures, and database systems that reinforced backend application design fundamentals.

education-bullet-se-bs-2.content: Finished a senior capstone centered on building and testing a multi-user web application with a small team.

education-bullet-se-ms-1.content: Current graduate coursework emphasizes distributed applications, advanced database systems, and software architecture tradeoffs for complex backend systems.

education-bullet-devops-bs-1.content: Focused on systems, networking, and operating systems coursework that built a strong foundation for automation and platform-oriented work.

education-bullet-devops-bs-2.content: Completed team-based lab projects that emphasized scripting, environment setup, and reliable delivery of working software.

education-bullet-devops-ms-1.content: Ongoing graduate study includes systems performance, automation-oriented lab work, and reliability-focused design topics that support platform engineering work.

education-bullet-cloud-bs-1.content: Completed coursework in distributed systems, networking, and security that supported later cloud infrastructure and platform responsibilities.

education-bullet-cloud-bs-2.content: Senior project work emphasized scalable system design and practical tradeoffs around reliability, performance, and maintainability.

education-bullet-cloud-ms-1.content: Current master's coursework focuses on cloud-scale distributed systems, network design, and infrastructure security concepts that map cleanly to AWS platform work.

project-bullet-se-northstar-1.content: Built TypeScript and Node.js services that unified audit evidence intake, reviewer workflows, and downstream compliance integrations for internal teams.

project-bullet-se-northstar-2.content: Designed background processing and API boundaries that reduced duplicate workflow logic while making failure handling easier to test and support.

project-bullet-se-blueridge-1.content: Created a backend integration service that normalized partner order events into a single internal contract for downstream commerce systems.

project-bullet-se-blueridge-2.content: Improved reconciliation and debugging by adding idempotent processing paths, clearer payload validation, and more useful operational logging.

project-bullet-se-personal-1.content: Built a personal trip-planning API with authentication, PostgreSQL-backed itinerary data, and automated tests for core scheduling flows.

project-bullet-se-personal-2.content: Packaged the service with Docker and documented a clean OpenAPI surface to practice maintainable backend delivery outside of work projects.

project-bullet-devops-northstar-1.content: Standardized GitHub Actions and Terraform deployment patterns so service teams could promote changes through shared environments with fewer manual steps.

project-bullet-devops-northstar-2.content: Added rollout checks, environment validation, and reusable release templates that improved self-service delivery without weakening operational guardrails.

project-bullet-devops-blueridge-1.content: Built a shared CI/CD baseline for commerce services that aligned container image handling, deployment steps, and rollback expectations across teams.

project-bullet-devops-blueridge-2.content: Centralized release telemetry in Datadog and CloudWatch so deployment health signals were easier to interpret during change windows.

project-bullet-devops-personal-1.content: Automated homelab cluster bootstrap, backup verification, and certificate rotation to practice repeatable operational workflows on a smaller scale.

project-bullet-devops-personal-2.content: Created lightweight monitoring and alert tests that made it easier to validate reliability changes before applying them to the running environment.

project-bullet-cloud-northstar-1.content: Improved shared AWS account foundations with clearer IAM boundaries, reusable Terraform modules, and more consistent networking patterns for platform services.

project-bullet-cloud-northstar-2.content: Expanded observability and cost visibility for shared infrastructure so teams could make more informed reliability and capacity decisions.

project-bullet-cloud-blueridge-1.content: Supported migration of checkout-related workloads toward ECS, RDS, and queue-backed processing patterns that were easier to scale and operate in AWS.

project-bullet-cloud-blueridge-2.content: Improved cloud-hosted service resilience through better alarms, resource tuning, and infrastructure conventions that reduced surprise production behavior.

project-bullet-cloud-personal-1.content: Built a small serverless reporting tool with Lambda, EventBridge, and S3 to summarize personal AWS usage and surface avoidable spend patterns.

project-bullet-cloud-personal-2.content: Defined the infrastructure in Terraform and used the project to explore practical tradeoffs around cloud visibility, automation, and low-cost operations.

additional-exp-bullet-se-1.content: Guided middle-school students through building simple full stack web apps, explaining how browser interactions, routes, and data storage fit together in practice.

additional-exp-bullet-se-2.content: Helped students debug JavaScript, HTML, and API integration issues while breaking larger web concepts into approachable workshop exercises.

additional-exp-bullet-devops-1.content: Introduced students to practical development workflow habits such as version control, repeatable setup steps, and the difference between local work and deployed applications.

additional-exp-bullet-devops-2.content: Built workshop material that covered lightweight deployment and automation concepts so students could see how small web projects move from code to usable environments.

additional-exp-bullet-cloud-1.content: Taught students the basics of how web applications are hosted, including the roles of servers, storage, networking, and cloud-managed services beyond local development.

additional-exp-bullet-cloud-2.content: Used age-appropriate examples to explain reliability, scalability, and platform fundamentals while helping students publish small projects to shared web environments.

job-atlas-health.compensationNotes: Open to bonus and equity if total package is competitive.
job-atlas-health.notes: Strong alignment with the Software Engineer profile. Referred by a former coworker.

job-meridian-labs.compensationNotes: Hybrid setup is workable if the offer lands near the top of the range or includes a meaningful annual bonus.
job-meridian-labs.notes: Submitted with the Software Engineer base profile and a tailored summary.

job-pinecrest-data.description: Role blending internal platform tooling with backend service development for analytics products.
job-pinecrest-data.compensationNotes: Would want the base near $165,000 given the blended platform and backend scope.
job-pinecrest-data.notes: Saved as an interested role to revisit after updating a few platform-related bullets.

job-cedarstack.description: Delivery automation and platform operations role with Terraform, Kubernetes, and AWS ownership.
job-cedarstack.compensationNotes: Base is close to target; bonus or equity would help close the gap.

job-nimbus-harbor.description: Senior DevOps role supporting multi-service deployments, platform tooling, and production readiness.
job-nimbus-harbor.compensationNotes: Offer is competitive on base, but still comparing bonus structure, equity, and benefits against other opportunities.
job-nimbus-harbor.notes: Received a written offer and requested a week to review compensation and team structure.

job-rivet-commerce.description: Reliability engineering role with on-call expectations, observability ownership, and deployment safeguards.
job-rivet-commerce.compensationNotes: Base range was workable, but the overall package did not stand out relative to other opportunities under consideration.
job-rivet-commerce.notes: Received a polite rejection after the first round.

job-summit-grid.description: AWS-heavy cloud engineering role focused on networking, IAM, Terraform, and platform resiliency.
job-summit-grid.compensationNotes: Would prefer the offer to include bonus or equity if the base lands below $175,000.

interview-atlas-screen.notes: Recruiter screen focused on healthcare domain familiarity and backend API experience.

interview-atlas-panel.notes: Panel with the engineering manager and a senior backend engineer. Good discussion about service reliability and testing strategy.

interview-cedarstack-manager.notes: Hiring manager interview about CI/CD ownership, deployment guardrails, and incident response habits.

interview-nimbus-screen.notes: Intro call with recruiter covering compensation, remote expectations, and process timing.

interview-nimbus-technical.notes: Technical round covering Terraform module design, Kubernetes rollout safety, and release automation tradeoffs.

interview-summit-architecture.notes: Cloud architecture discussion focused on AWS networking, IAM boundaries, and observability patterns.

interview-orchard-final.notes: Final loop completed; strong conversation on platform ownership and internal tooling priorities.

interview-skylane-next.notes: Recruiter indicated the next step will be a platform architecture discussion with the reliability manager.

question-atlas-1.question: Describe a backend system you improved for reliability.
question-atlas-1.answer: I described how I improved an internal workflow service by tightening integration contracts, expanding automated tests, and documenting failure handling so the team could resolve issues faster.

question-cedarstack-1.question: How have you reduced deployment risk in production environments?
question-cedarstack-1.answer: I highlighted pipeline guardrails such as progressive rollout checks, environment validation, and clearer rollback paths in GitHub Actions and Kubernetes deployment workflows.

question-bluepeak-1.question: Describe your experience with AWS networking and identity management.
question-bluepeak-1.answer: I explained my experience working with VPC design, security groups, IAM roles and policies, and Terraform-based infrastructure patterns that help teams use cloud resources safely.

question-harborline-1.question: What metrics do you use to evaluate CI/CD health?
question-harborline-1.answer: I usually focus on lead time, change failure rate, deployment frequency, pipeline success trends, and the time it takes teams to recover from failed releases.

question-northfield-1.question: Tell us about a time you simplified a complex integration.
question-northfield-1.answer: I wrote about consolidating multiple fragile integration steps into a smaller, better-tested service boundary with clearer retries, observability, and ownership.
